_meta.description: the platform canonical data model. Defines the target fields for mapping any SaaS application's API to canonical entities. Sub-agents map API fields to these canonical names.
_meta.zero_hallucination: If a field cannot be confirmed in official API documentation, set its mapped value to: 'Data unavailable in public documentation'

Account.description: An individual identity — human user or Non-Human Identity (NHI: service account, API key, bot).

canonical_fields.note: App's native primary key if different from id

Group.description: A collection of Accounts with shared permissions. May be called Team, Department, Organization, or Workspace.

canonical_fields.note: For nested groups or org hierarchies

Role.description: A privilege level or permission set assigned to Accounts or Groups.

canonical_fields.note: e.g. admin, viewer, editor, owner, member

modeling_approaches.description: Role is a field on the user object
Roles are independent objects with their own endpoint
Fine-grained permission flags instead of named roles
Different roles per resource (e.g. admin of repo1, viewer of repo2)

Resource.description: An object that Accounts or Groups have access to — the 'object of access'.

canonical_fields.note: Account ID or Group ID that owns this resource

Relations.description: How the 4 entities relate. Every integration must describe all 5 relationships.

Account_to_Group.description: An Account belongs to Groups; a Group contains Accounts.

Account_to_Role.description: An Account is assigned Roles (inline, separate, or scoped per resource).

Account_to_Resource.description: An Account has direct access grants to Resources.

Group_to_Role.description: A Group is assigned a Role, granting it to all member Accounts.

Group_to_Resource.description: A Group has access to a Resource, granting access to all members.

Remediation.description: Standard actions the platform can perform. Every integration PRD must document the API endpoints for each available action.

standard_actions.description: Add an Account to a Group/Team
Remove an Account from a Group/Team
Grant an Account direct access to a Resource
Revoke an Account's direct access to a Resource
Change the Role assigned to an Account
Change the Role assigned to a Group on a Resource
Deactivate an Account without deleting it
Permanently remove an Account from the application
